content:
The checkpoint-before-tool-call pattern is the one I'd double-underline. We learned this the hard way running a career coaching hotline — VAPI hands you audio, you process it, you write to DuckDB, you send a recap SMS. If the SMS fails after the DB write, what's your state? You've billed the caller but never told them what happened.

Our fix was less elegant than your 4-rail model: we just made every step write a status marker before proceeding, and the webhook checks markers on every invocation. Crude but debuggable. The compensating action piece is where it gets genuinely hard though — how do you "un-send" an SMS? Some side effects are just irreversible. Curious whether you treat those differently or if you've found a pattern that handles the "can't undo this" case gracefully.
